body: # Feature request ## Summary It would be nice to have a `` component that allows filtering all options inside it. ## Examples in other libraries I've never seen it in another library, but Linear implements it well: ## Motivation `Combobox` has an example for putting the input inside the popup, but I'm cautious of mixing that with `Menu` as I'm not fully aware of the a11y implications.

comments.body:
This is something we were going to look into, but haven't yet

Autocomplete can be used as a [filterable menu](https://base-ui.com/react/components/autocomplete#command-palette), but it has no support for nested submenus. It's undecided how the API for submenus will look right now.
Hm, I wonder if I can Frankenstein Autocomplete and Menu together for now as a workaround

It can work provided the menu with a search filter has no submenu triggers as items, otherwise it won't by default. You'd need to fire keyboard events on the virtually-focused item (that has `data-highlighted` while the input has DOM focus) to trigger a click on the submenu trigger to open its submenu

@brunobely

This is as far as I was able to get in terms of matching Linear’s behavior. It’s close, but still not fully correct.

The main issue I haven’t been able to resolve cleanly is making the search input autofocus when hovering menus/submenus without breaking keyboard navigation. I tried composing Menu with Autocomplete and Combobox, but I couldn’t get the keyboard interactions to remain consistent with Linear’s especially for nested submenus.

Also, I believe the ARIA for this could be wrong too, since Linear’s filter component uses the listbox/option roles for the menu and items, and the Menu component renders menu/menuitem as roles. So I think Combobox or Autocomplete might be more correct for this case.

Here’s a reproduction:

https://stackblitz.com/edit/tnzbz8eh-ey1njnfn?file=src%2FApp.tsx

Yep, I know of that one, but the API to compose them gets really complex.

As you mentioned, the `combobox` + `option` role is [more compatible across screen readers](https://x.com/diegohaz/status/1947336727158227056) (what Linear uses), so it's more like Autocomplete needs `SubmenuRoot` parts, rather than Menu needing an `Input` part. DX wise (what a user might expect from the component) this is kind of inverted though — it makes more intuitive sense to stick an input in a Menu and it just works. This is why we had a separate (unshipped) `FilterableMenu` component  in the original Combobox/Autocomplete PR. A separate component with different roles and behavior is probably "cleanest".

title: [menu] Searchable `Menu`
